benefit_assessment_plain_language: M4u still does not prove end-to-end Flye GPU speedup: this DGX run was CPU baseline 84s versus batch/cache substitution 91s. It does show a real seam-overhead improvement: selected substitution worker-process average fell from M4t 414.343489 ms to M4u 220.396566 ms, because one later selected query reused the verified batch output with worker_process_ms=0. The next ROI target is to make the batch/cache path cover more graph-facing substitutions or move to a true persistent worker.

allowed: M4u session-batch mode can defer an early selected supported query, run one validated batch worker request, and later substitute from the verified batch cache.
Positive toy-raw final Flye artifacts matched CPU while one substitution came from the batch run and one came from the batch cache.
Selected substitution worker-process and seam-total average timing improved versus M4t.
Mismatch and unsupported selected-shape negative paths still failed closed.
forbidden: No claim that all selected query occurrences can safely reuse cached output without exact CPU comparison.
